name: Hello Grok
tagline: The simplest possible Grok agent — single agent, single tool, under 20 lines of YAML.
description: A minimal starter template that demonstrates the canonical grok-agent.yaml shape: one runtime, one tool, one approval gate. Ideal as the first install when you're learning the platform or onboarding a teammate.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: hello-grok
  name: Hello Grok
spec:
  runtime: grok-4-fast
  safety:
    policy: standard
  tools:
    - id: echo
      uses: core.echo
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: Your first Grok-native agent in twenty lines.
visuals.subheadline: Clone, customise, ship — the canonical starter template.

name: Reply Engagement Bot
tagline: Watches your X mentions and drafts thoughtful, on-brand replies behind an approval gate.
description: Polls your X mentions, scores each one for relevance, then drafts a reply in your voice. Nothing posts without your tap — the approval queue lives in the CLI and the X composer. Built on the strict safety profile.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: reply-engagement-bot
spec:
  runtime: grok-4-fast
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: watch-mentions
      uses: x.mentions.poll
    - id: draft-reply
      uses: x.compose
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: Never miss a mention. Never auto-post a regret.
visuals.subheadline: Approval-gated drafts in your voice, generated the moment a mention lands.

name: Trend To Thread
tagline: Monitors X trends and drafts a full thread on topics that match your profile.
description: Subscribes to your topical filters, watches the trending API, and drafts a calibrated thread the moment a relevant topic crosses your threshold. Threads land in the approval queue with sources attached — never auto-posted.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: trend-to-thread
spec:
  runtime: grok-4
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: watch-trends
      uses: x.trends.subscribe
    - id: draft-thread
      uses: x.compose.thread
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: Catch the trend. Ship the thread. Approve the post.
visuals.subheadline: Topical filters, calibrated drafts, sources attached — all behind one approval tap.

name: Research Swarm
tagline: A three-agent swarm: researcher gathers sources, critic challenges them, publisher writes the brief.
description: Coordinates three specialist agents under the Swarm-Ready v1 spec. Researcher pulls citations, Critic stress-tests them, Publisher produces the final brief. Every hop is logged for audit and replay.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Swarm
metadata:
  id: research-swarm
spec:
  agents:
    - id: researcher
      role: retriever
      runtime: grok-4
    - id: critic
      role: arbiter
      runtime: grok-4
    - id: publisher
      role: composer
      runtime: grok-4-fast
  routing:
    - from: researcher
      to: critic
      always: true
    - from: critic
      to: publisher
      when: "confidence >= 0.7"
  audit:
    log: true

visuals.headline: Three agents, one brief. Every claim challenged.
visuals.subheadline: Researcher → Critic → Publisher, with every hop logged for audit and replay.

name: Code Reviewer
tagline: Reviews GitHub PRs with Grok reasoning. Posts inline comments behind an approval gate.
description: Subscribes to your repository's PR webhook, runs a structured review pass, and queues inline comments for your approval. Ships with a curated rule pack for security, perf, and style — every flag links to a citation.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: code-reviewer
spec:
  runtime: grok-4
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: read-pr
      uses: github.pulls.read
    - id: post-review
      uses: github.pulls.review
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: PR reviews that cite their sources.
visuals.subheadline: Inline comments, queued for your approval — security, perf, and style in one pass.

name: Thread Ghostwriter
tagline: Turns a rough idea into a polished, tone-matched X thread ready for approval.
description: Drop a paragraph, pick a tone, get a structured thread back with hook, body, and CTA. Tone-matching learns from your last 200 posts; everything lands in the approval queue with diff against your house style guide.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: thread-ghostwriter
spec:
  runtime: grok-4
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: tone-match
      uses: x.history.read
    - id: draft-thread
      uses: x.compose.thread
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: Idea in. Thread out. Voice intact.
visuals.subheadline: Tone-matched against your last 200 posts, diffed against your house style guide.

name: Personal Knowledge
tagline: Builds a persistent, searchable memory of your X history. Ask what you've posted, liked, or been mentioned in.
description: Indexes your X archive into a private vector store. Ask natural-language questions and get back exact post citations with permalinks. Encrypted at rest and never leaves your account scope.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: personal-knowledge
spec:
  runtime: grok-4
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: index-archive
      uses: x.archive.read
    - id: query-memory
      uses: vector.query
  approval:
    required: false

visuals.headline: Your X history, finally searchable.
visuals.subheadline: Natural-language queries, exact-post citations, encrypted at rest.

name: Scientific Discovery
tagline: Watches arXiv plus the X discussion. Produces a daily brief of what's new and what researchers are saying.
description: Cross-references new arXiv submissions in your tracked categories with the X conversation around them. Returns a daily digest with novelty score, sentiment, and the most-cited reply threads — so you see signal, not noise.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: scientific-discovery
spec:
  runtime: grok-4
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: pull-arxiv
      uses: arxiv.fetch
    - id: cross-x
      uses: x.search
  approval:
    required: false

visuals.headline: arXiv plus X — distilled into one daily brief.
visuals.subheadline: Novelty score, sentiment, top reply threads. Signal only.

name: Voice Agent X
tagline: Speak a post, review the transcript, approve, publish. Voice-first X workflow with safety gates.
description: Hands-free composition for X. Wake-word activation, barge-in, and a structured grammar for replies, DMs, and threads — every utterance lands as a transcript in the approval queue before anything ships.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: VoiceAgent
metadata:
  id: voice-agent-x
spec:
  wakeWord: "hey grok"
  language: en-US
  barge-in: true
  commands:
    - intent: post
      grammar: "post <text>"
    - intent: reply
      grammar: "reply to <handle> saying <text>"
    - intent: dm
      grammar: "dm <handle> <text>"
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: Talk to X. Approve before it ships.
visuals.subheadline: Wake-word, barge-in, structured grammar — every post passes the transcript review.

name: Live Event Commentator
tagline: Real-time event commentary on X with strict rate limits and a kill switch.
description: Streams an event feed (sports, conference, product launch) and drafts running commentary at the cadence you set. Hard rate limit, hard kill switch, every post gated on approval. Engineered for the strictest safety profile.

yaml.content:
apiVersion: grokinstall.dev/v1
kind: Agent
metadata:
  id: live-event-commentator
spec:
  runtime: grok-4-fast
  safety:
    policy: safety-max
  tools:
    - id: stream-feed
      uses: events.subscribe
    - id: draft-commentary
      uses: x.compose
  rateLimit:
    perMinute: 4
  killSwitch:
    keyword: "abort"
  approval:
    required: true

visuals.headline: Live commentary. Hard limits. One kill switch.
visuals.subheadline: Streams events into draft posts at your cadence — every line gated on approval.
